plang_failures_are_pre_existing.cause: Stale .pr files in legacy tests/modifiers/ directory built against older error.handle / goal.call schema (Actor: 'this' literal, Goal: {name} param shape). Maintained Tests/Modules/Modifiers/ versions pass.

moderate_coverage_files: PLang/App/modules/list/add.cs (56.1% — complex-snapshot path uncovered, see finding #2)
PLang/App/Utils/Json.cs (54.2% — only ~5 new lines, covered indirectly)
PLang/App/Debug/this.cs (44.3% — only ~5 lines changed by coder/v1)

findings.issue: MintTyped if-chain has 5 cold-type arms (decimal, float, DateTimeOffset, Guid, byte[]) plus a reflection cold-fallback that are all uncovered. Only string, int, long, double, bool, DateTime, List, Dict are tested.
findings.impact: A regression in any of the 5 uncovered type arms (e.g. byte[] taking IList branch instead) would ship silently. The Activator.CreateInstance reflection path is the most fragile spot and untested.
findings.suggestion: Add Set_DecimalValue_MintsDataOfDecimal, Set_FloatValue_MintsDataOfFloat, Set_DateTimeOffsetValue_MintsDataOfDateTimeOffset, Set_GuidValue_MintsDataOfGuid, Set_ByteArrayValue_MintsDataOfByteArray, plus one custom-record test exercising the reflection fallback. Pattern identical to existing Set_DateTimeValue_MintsDataOfDateTime.

findings.issue: Lines 53–66 (the complex-snapshot path via Data.SnapshotClone with UnwrapJsonElement) are entirely uncovered. All existing list.add tests use string values that take the cheap-clone IConvertible branch (line 50). The catch-fallback for JsonException/NotSupportedException (lines 59–65) is also uncovered.
findings.impact: This is exactly the path codeanalyzer/v2 flagged as a quiet behavior unification (UnwrapJsonElement now applies to list-entry snapshots). A regression to JsonElement leaks downstream goes silent. The fallback alias-mode is undefended.
findings.suggestion: Add Add_DictValue_SnapshotCloneUnwrapsJsonElement that calls list.add with a Dictionary<string, object?> value containing nested Dictionary, then asserts the stored entry's Value is Dictionary<string, object?> (NOT JsonElement) at all depths.

findings.issue: The Variables.Set dot-path now goes through Data.SnapshotClone (UnwrapJsonElement applied). The existing Set_DotPath_ConvertsListOfObject_ToTypedList passes a dict-list and verifies properties survive — but that works under BOTH the old (JsonElement leak via TypeMapping) and new (Dictionary via UnwrapJsonElement) shapes.
findings.impact: Codeanalyzer/v2's behavioral unification is unprotected at this site. Regression to JsonElement graph passes the existing dot-path tests.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) add a unit-level test SnapshotClone_OfNestedDict_UnwrapsJsonElementsAtAllDepths on Data.SnapshotClone directly (faster, simpler), or (b) add a dot-path test that introspects the converted intermediate type before downstream conversion.

findings.issue: Two error paths uncovered: (a) Run() Unknown forced-type ServiceError (lines 68–70) — no test passes type='bogus'. (b) ValidateBuild conversion-failure error message (line 37) — no unit test for ValidateBuild. Existing Set_ForcedType_ConversionFailure_ReturnsError checks the runtime path but only asserts Success==false (weak).
findings.impact: A breaking change to error key 'UnknownType', the 400 status code, or the message format would slip through. Per false_green_techniques.md, Success==false is weak — Error.Key + StatusCode should be pinned.
findings.suggestion: Add Set_ForcedType_UnknownTypeName_ReturnsServiceError (assert Error.Key=='UnknownType', StatusCode==400) and ValidateBuild_ForcedTypeConversionFailure_ReturnsErrorString (unit test). Strengthen existing Set_ForcedType_ConversionFailure_ReturnsError to also pin Error.Key and StatusCode.

findings.issue: Set_IntValue_MintsDataOfInt accepts EITHER Data<int> OR Data<long> (because the JSON pipeline normalizes int to long). The permissive assertion means a regression to e.g. Data<short> or Data<byte> would still pass.
findings.impact: Low — the long arm is strictly tested by the separate Set_LongValue_MintsDataOfLong, so the integer hot path is partly pinned. But the int test name is misleading.
findings.suggestion: Either rename to Set_IntOrLongValue_MintsTypedNumeric, delete (redundant with Set_LongValue), or pin to a specific shape that the JSON pipeline cannot drift away from.

findings.issue: No test pins that prev.OnDelete is NOT fired during a Set replacement. Architect's contract: OnDelete is for Remove only; replacement fires only OnChange.
findings.impact: Low — the dumb-storage replacement code is correct today; but if a future change started firing OnDelete on prev disposal (a plausible 'cleanup' refactor), no test would catch it.
findings.suggestion: One-liner addition to Set_Replace_FiresOnChange_OnPrev_WithDvAsNewData: subscribe deleteCalls counter on prev.OnDelete; assert deleteCalls == 0 after Set(dv).

findings.issue: Two PLang tests in legacy tests/modifiers/ have stale .pr files built against an older error.handle / goal.call schema (Actor: 'this' literal, Goal: {name} parameter). They fail with TypeMismatch(400) at runtime. Maintained Tests/Modules/Modifiers/ versions pass.
findings.impact: Out of coder/v1 scope. Not a regression. But inflates the failed-count and could mask real future regressions in PLang test runs.
findings.suggestion: Either delete tests/modifiers/.build/ and rebuild via plang build, or delete the legacy directory entirely if Tests/Modules/Modifiers/ supersedes it.

summary: C# 2524/2533 with 9 deferred stubs honestly failing — confirmed. PLang 170/173 with 2 fails in legacy tests/modifiers/ and 1 unrelated stale entry — not regressions. Identity-preservation contract is strongly pinned (every aliasing claim uses ReferenceEquals on Properties / event lists / Value). The pivot from architect's 'alias prev events on Set' to Ingi's 'dumb storage' is correctly reflected in SubscriberSurvivalTests. AsCanonical live-variable resolution and IsPlangAssignable single-source-of-truth are tested. 4 major + 3 minor findings, all coverage gaps (no false greens). Recommend approve-with-deferred-coverage OR brief coder bounce for findings #1–#4.
